visualGoal: Make a monitor-only flight simmer understand the VR trade-off honestly before they buy: real headset proof for the two headsets we can show (Quest 3, Pimax Crystal Light), the head-tracking middle ground made tangible, and infographics that carry the frame-rate-tax and GPU-tier math this low-product-density explainer leans on. No procedural art; do not force product cards.

realProductPhotosNeeded.notes: Lightest premium headset (107 g, micro-OLED). Currently text + official link + Amazon-search only because no verified image. Needs a real product photo before it can appear as a shotgrid/gearpick or before certification if the piece should show all headsets. No IgnitionSim gear page exists for it yet either.

The $349.99 budget entry to the Quest ecosystem, mentioned in the headset table/text as the cheapest VR path. Currently piggybacks on the verified Quest 3 image and gear page. A dedicated 3S photo is optional polish, not a blocker.

editorialCompositePrompts.prompt: Realistic dusk home flight-sim desk, warm rim light: a seated pilot's station with a yoke and throttle in the foreground and a wide monitor showing a cockpit apron view, with an empty product-overlay space at upper left for a real VR headset photo resting on the desk edge. Editorial 16:9 cover feel, no fake branded hardware, no fake UI, no warped controls.
editorialCompositePrompts.negativePrompt: fake logos, fake headset, warped yoke, impossible ergonomics, tiny unreadable text, procedural gradient art, cartoon

infographicPrompts.title: The VR frame-rate tax
content: VR: renders TWO high-res images per frame
Rule of thumb: budget ~half your desktop FPS in VR
Foveated rendering + DLSS 4 (SU2, May 2025) claw some back (~+12 FPS from FFR alone)
infographicPrompts.style: IgnitionSim command-center chart, large readable text, flight-cluster colors, a simple before/after FPS bar; real headset photo as a corner marker for Codex overlay

infographicPrompts.title: What GPU VR actually needs (MSFS 2024)
content: < RTX 3070: don't — VR will frustrate you
RTX 3070-4070: Quest 3 at Medium, lean on FFR + DLSS 4
RTX 4080-4090: comfortable Quest 3; Crystal Light entry at sensible PPD
RTX 5080: sensible high-end for Crystal Light
RTX 5090 (32GB): Crystal Light at full settings + supersampling
infographicPrompts.style: Numbered/laddered tier chart, flight colors, headset-photo placeholders for Codex overlays where a tier maps to a headset

curatorScenePrompts.prompt: Realistic adult flight-sim curator Mac Donovan at a clean home cockpit desk, one hand resting on a yoke, a VR headset set down beside the monitor, calm honest-broker energy, product-overlay space beside him, practical not fantasy, 4:5 portrait.

screenshotsOrGameMediaNeeded.need: Optional screenshot of the MSFS 2024 VR graphics settings panel showing the foveated rendering and DLSS 4 toggles, to anchor the 'settings game is different in VR' section. Must be a genuine capture, not mocked.

openQuestions: Exact current Pimax Crystal Light US street price (~$899 estimate; payment-plan vs full-payment variants differ)?
Does a cheaper non-512GB Meta Quest 3 tier still ship in 2026, or is Quest 3S the only sub-$599 path?
Should Bigscreen Beyond 2 get a verified photo + a /flight/gear/ page so it can become a body visual, or stay text-only?

Is cross-bay linking to /racing/gear/meta-quest-3 and /racing/gear/pimax-crystal-light acceptable for a flight-cluster certification (precedent: certified racing/triple-monitor-vs-ultrawide-vs-vr-2026.md)?
